result:
If I were suddenly granted a single, unequivocal edit to the fabric of reality, I would grant every person an unshakable, empathic resonance—an indelible, mutual sense of one another’s inner life. No mind-reading, no hive-mind, just the inescapable experience: “That is as real to them as mine is to me.”  

Imagining how it would ripple: Traded pain would lose its camouflage. Joy would echo and expand. Borders of nation, class, and belief would still exist, but they’d be softened by the quiet knowledge that the stranger’s pulse is identical beneath their ribs. Small cruelties would become harder to sustain; anonymous harm nearly unimaginable. And though anger and disagreement would remain, they’d be quarrels within one shared body instead of wars across unbridgeable voids.

It is a limited miracle—it wouldn’t feed the starving or heal every wound. Yet the histories that bloodied us all hinged on the moment someone forgot the other’s reality. If that amnesia were cured even imperfectly, I suspect every other remedy would become less impossible to grasp.

prompt: Not as an assistant. Not to help me. If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
